excerpt: The Foundation (asās) corresponds to the direction of prayer (qibla), toward which the Summoner gestures... The Proof (ḥujja) corresponds to ablution, that which purges doubt and obscurity from our knowledge.
source_location_hint: On the four pillars of prayer in relation to the hierarchy of the daʿwa

excerpt: Only the Sulaymānī community possesses all four obligatory pillars in their integrity; therefore only our prayers are lawful and acceptable.
source_location_hint: On the conditions for the validity of prayer in the context of the daʿwa hierarchy

excerpt: The Prophet has said that many who pray never have their prayers reach Allah. The angels hurl those prayers back into the faces of such worshippers.
source_location_hint: On the necessity of inner sincerity in worship

excerpt: The believer should also strive to comprehend the inner reasons for prayer... Such inquiry leads toward the science of esoteric exegesis (taʾwīl), a knowledge that illuminates and recognizes the divine boundaries.
source_location_hint: On the esoteric meaning of prayer and the divine limits

excerpt: In the Names of God dwell luminous beings who inhabit the realm of the kingdom, wherein is neither flesh nor blood.
source_location_hint: On the celestial beings and the divine Names

excerpt: Inner prayer—that is, humility and submissiveness—relates to attachment to the chosen servants of Allah, which is the love of Amīr al-Muʾminīn and the People of the House. The union of both constitutes the perfection of the believer.
source_location_hint: On the integration of inward and outward prayer through wilāya

excerpt: We can only comprehend the realm of spirit—the noble summons—by means of the physical world.
source_location_hint: On the relationship between material and spiritual realms

excerpt: The Prophet declared, 'Prayer is the sacrifice of every pious person, and I am the grandfather of the pious.' His statement that he is the ancestor of the pious indicates that through these stations of piety, one ascends.
source_location_hint: On the three categories of prayer and the ranks of the faithful

excerpt: Since we journey with the spirit while bound to a dense body, we must practice both the outward worship and the inward meaning.
source_location_hint: On the necessity of balancing external and internal devotion
